perturbation.original_text: You hereby agree that neither you nor your Representatives will use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws. Return or Destruction of Documents If you determine that you do not wish to proceed with a Transaction or your evaluation thereof, you will promptly advise the Company and Stone Key in writing of that decision.
perturbation.changed_text: Return or Destruction of Documents If you determine that you do not wish to proceed with a Transaction or your evaluation thereof, you will promptly advise the Company and Stone Key in writing of that decision. You hereby agree that neither you nor your Representatives will use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the statement on securities law compliance to the 'Return or Destruction of Documents' section. The prohibition on trading on inside information is generally placed in a separate section.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Section 10(b) and Rule 10b-5
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 and Rule 10b-5 prohibit the use of manipulative and deceptive devices in connection with the purchase or sale of securities. Placing the warning about insider trading within the section about returning documents could be seen as downplaying its importance, potentially leading to a weaker defense against claims of insider trading due to lack of clear and prominent disclosure.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.10b-5 - Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. CFR prev | next § 240.10b-5 Employment of manipulative and deceptive devices. It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce, or of the mails or of any facility of any national securities exchange , (a) To employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, (b) To make any untrue statement of a material fact or to omit to state a material fact necessary in order to make the statements made, in the light of the circumstances under which they were made, not misleading, or (c) To engage in any act, practice, or course of business which operates or would operate as a fraud or deceit upon any person, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security . (Sec. 10; 48 Stat. 891 ; 15 U.S.C. 78j ) [ 13 FR 8183 , Dec. 22, 1948, as amended at 16 FR 7928 , Aug. 11, 1951] CFR Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The relocation of the clause concerning compliance with securities laws to the section about document return creates a structural flaw, potentially diminishing its prominence and impact. This arrangement could weaken the defense against insider trading claims due to inadequate disclosure. Scraped Law Snippet 1, specifically 17 CFR § 240.10b-5, explicitly prohibits the use of manipulative and deceptive devices in connection with the purchase or sale of securities. While the modified text doesn't eliminate the clause, its placement within a less relevant section could be interpreted as downplaying its significance, potentially leading to a violation of this rule. The change falls under Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction since a legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section, making it difficult for parties to interpret.
